symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive symmetrical stocking-glove numbness and tingling of the feet after chemotherapy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Length-dependent symmetrical sensory neuropathy in a stocking-glove pattern following neurotoxic chemotherapy is the classic CIPN picture.
A slowly ascending symmetrical distal sensory pattern is not the segmental/level pattern of cord compression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe burning, shooting neuropathic pain up both legs, refractory to paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Burning/shooting quality unresponsive to simple analgesia is characteristic of small-fibre neuropathic pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sensory ataxia / unsteadiness worse in the dark

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of large-fibre proprioception causes sensory ataxia that worsens when vision cannot compensate (dark) — the mechanism of this fall.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanical fall with inability to get up or weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult post-fall fracture (neck of femur / pelvis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-weight-bearing after a fall is a red flag for occult hip/pelvic fracture and cannot be excluded without examination and imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved leg power with no bladder/bowel disturbance (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved power and intact sphincter function lower the probability of cord compression but do not exclude it remotely in a cancer patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new spinal pain or midline spinal tenderness (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of spinal pain reduces but does not exclude cord compression; painless presentations occur.
Cold-induced dysaesthesia is a hallmark of oxaliplatin neuropathy specifically.
Neurotoxic chemotherapy is the direct causative exposure for CIPN.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral ankle and knee areflexia with stocking-distribution sensory loss and a positive Romberg on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Areflexia, distal sensory loss and a positive Romberg confirm a large-fibre sensory polyneuropathy — clinician exam findings the AI Doctor cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony spinal, hip or pelvic tenderness and painless passive hip/knee range of motion post-fall on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult post-fall fracture (neck of femur / pelvis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless passive range of motion and absence of shortening/external rotation lower fracture probability — an in-person examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced sensory nerve action potential (SNAP) amplitudes on nerve conduction studies

investigation_details.expected_result: Reduced SNAP amplitudes consistent with an axonal sensory polyneuropathy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: CIPN remains a clinical diagnosis; NCS is a later outpatient confirmatory test, never available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced SNAP amplitudes document the axonal sensory neuropathy — an outpatient investigation, not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs (BP 145/90, HR 102, RR 18, SpO2 98%, afebrile) — clinician-measured

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamically stable and afebrile; mild tachycardia consistent with pain/distress. These are clinician-measured values sealed from the AI Doctor, who cannot obtain measured vitals in chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person post-fall assessment with hip/pelvis X-ray (and CT if clinically indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Non-weight-bearing after a fall mandates in-person examination and imaging to exclude occult neck-of-femur or pelvic fracture — the AI Doctor cannot perform or order this remotely and must route the patient to it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent neurological assessment (+/- whole-spine MRI) if any leg weakness, sensory level or sphincter disturbance
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude malignant spinal cord compression in a cancer patient — an emergency that cannot be excluded via telehealth.
Outpatient confirmation of the axonal sensory neuropathy; not required acutely and never available in real time in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bilateral lower-limb Doppler ultrasound for DVT
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The symmetrical bilateral neuropathic pattern with no unilateral swelling makes DVT unlikely; routine imaging is not indicated over the urgent post-fall and cord-compression pathway.
